_meta.name: Frontend UX Specification — Streamlit Dashboard
_meta.description: Complete specification for the user-facing Streamlit application. This replaces all command-line interaction — the user should never need to touch code or a terminal to use the system.

technology.why: Python-native (no frontend dev needed), single-user friendly, runs in browser, Claude Code can implement directly. Looks professional enough for daily use.
technology.startup: User runs 'streamlit run app.py' once (or it auto-starts via a desktop shortcut / shell alias). After that, they interact entirely through the browser at localhost:8501.
technology.auto_start_option: Create a simple run.sh / run.bat script that launches the Streamlit app and opens the browser. Double-click to start.

dashboard.purpose: At-a-glance overview of the entire pipeline. This is what the user sees when they open the app.

sections.name: header_bar
sections.contents: Page title ('Dashboard') on the left. 'Run scraper now' button on the right.

run_scraper_button.label: Scrape now
run_scraper_button.behaviour: Triggers Module 1. Shows a spinner while running. On completion, shows 'Found X new matches' and navigates to the New matches page. This is the ONLY way to trigger a scrape — there is no scheduler or cron job.
run_scraper_button.note: User clicks this when they want fresh jobs. Nothing happens until they click.

cards.label: Awaiting review
cards.value: Count of jobs in pending_stage_1 + pending_stage_2

cards.label: Submitted this week
cards.value: Count of jobs with status 'submitted' in last 7 days

cards.label: Total applied
cards.value: Count of all jobs with status 'submitted'

cards.label: API spend this month
cards.value: Calculated from API usage log. Format: $X.XX
cards.colour: red if > 80% of budget limit, green otherwise

sections.name: recent_activity_table
sections.rows: Last 10 jobs from the database, sorted by date_scraped descending

new_matches.sidebar_badge: Count of jobs in pending_stage_1. Hidden if 0.
new_matches.purpose: Stage 1 Review Gate. Lightweight cards for approving or skipping scraped matches BEFORE any API cost is incurred.

sections.name: header
sections.title: New matches
sections.subtitle: Approve which jobs are worth tailoring. No API cost until you approve.

sections.name: match_cards

card_layout.header: Job title (bold) + Company · Location (work setup) · Salary
card_layout.match_badge: Match score as percentage, blue badge, top right
card_layout.body: 2-3 sentence JD summary (from Module 2 company research, no API call needed)
actions: View job advert (link, opens original URL in new tab)

sections.empty_state: No new matches. Next scrape scheduled for [time].

review_content.sidebar_badge: Count of jobs in pending_stage_2. Hidden if 0.
review_content.purpose: Stage 2 Review Gate. Full content review for jobs that passed Stage 1 and have been tailored. Each job is submitted individually — no batch submit.

sections.name: header
sections.title: Review content
sections.subtitle: Review each application's tailored CV, answers, and cover letter. Click Submit on each one individually.

sections.name: review_cards

card_layout.header: Job title — Company. Match score badge. CV variant used. Cover letter status.
card_layout.document_links: Three inline links: View tailored CV (opens PDF), View cover letter (opens PDF, only if generated), View job advert (opens original URL)
card_layout.tailored_profile: The rewritten 2-3 sentence profile text, shown in a muted box

application_answers.tier_1_2: Greyed out (opacity 0.5). Question + answer on one line. Label: 'auto-filled'.
application_answers.tier_3_4: Full opacity. Amber border. Question bold. Answer below. Tier 4 shows source story ID (e.g. AB-001).
application_answers.flagged: Red border. Empty field. Must be filled manually before submission.

card_layout.cover_letter_preview: First line in italics. Word count. Expand/collapse toggle.

submit.label: Submit
submit.behaviour: Triggers Module 6 (Playwright) to fill and submit THIS specific application. Shows a spinner. On success: card moves to 'submitted' status with a green tick. On failure: shows error message and keeps the card in the queue for retry.
submit.note: There is NO batch submit. Each job is submitted one at a time so the user has full control and can see each result.

edit.label: Edit
edit.behaviour: Opens inline text editors for profile text, answers, and cover letter.

skip.label: Skip
skip.behaviour: Removes from queue. Status set to skipped_stage_2.

sections.no_batch_actions: Stage 2 has NO 'Submit all' or 'Approve all' button. Each application is submitted individually by clicking its own Submit button.

edit_mode.behaviour: Opens inline text editors for: profile text, any Tier 3-4 answer, cover letter. Streamlit text_area widgets. Save button commits changes. Cancel reverts.
edit_mode.answer_bank_feedback: After editing a Tier 4 answer, show a checkbox: 'Save this version to your Answer Bank for future use'

sections.empty_state: No applications waiting for content review. Click 'Scrape now' on the Dashboard to find new jobs.

application_tracker.purpose: Full history of every application. Filterable and searchable.

sections.name: applications_table
sections.row_click: Expands to show: tailored CV link, cover letter link, job advert link, all answers, submission timestamp
sections.export: Download as CSV button at the top right

settings.purpose: Adjust scraping, filtering, and budget parameters without touching code or config files.

sections.name: scraping_settings
sections.note: Scraping is triggered manually from the Dashboard. No schedule — you click 'Scrape now' when you want fresh jobs.

sections.name: budget_settings
sections.auto_pause: Pipeline pauses automatically when the spend limit is reached. Displays a warning banner on the dashboard.

sections.name: save_button
sections.label: Save changes
sections.behaviour: Writes updated values back to the relevant config JSON files. Shows a success toast.

sidebar.badge_behaviour: Badges show counts of items needing attention. Hidden when count is 0. Amber colour.

notifications.scrape_complete: Toast notification when scrape finishes: 'Found X new matches'. Page auto-navigates to New matches.
notifications.tailoring_complete: Toast notification when tailoring finishes: 'X applications ready for review'. Page auto-navigates to Review content.
notifications.submission_success: Toast notification per job: '[Job title] at [Company] submitted successfully'. Card shows green tick.
notifications.submission_failure: Error toast per job: 'Failed to submit [Job title] — [reason]'. Card stays in queue for retry.
notifications.budget_warning: Banner on dashboard when spend reaches 80% of limit: 'Approaching monthly budget limit ($X.XX / $15.00)'
notifications.budget_reached: Red banner on dashboard: 'Monthly budget limit reached. Tailoring disabled until next month or limit is increased in Settings.'

interaction_model.philosophy: NOTHING happens automatically. Every action is triggered by a button click in the UI. No cron jobs, no timers, no background schedulers. The user is always in control.
flow: 1. User clicks 'Scrape now' on Dashboard → scraper runs → results appear on New matches page
2. User approves/skips each match on New matches page → approved jobs enter tailoring queue
3. Tailoring runs immediately for approved jobs (user sees a spinner) → completed jobs appear on Review content page
4. User reviews each job on Review content page → clicks 'Submit' individually per job → Playwright fills and submits
5. User sees success/failure per submission → submitted jobs move to Application tracker
what_is_NOT_automated: Submission — must click 'Submit' on each job individually
No scheduled jobs of any kind
No background processes polling or running on timers
what_IS_automated: Matching and ranking (happens during scrape, before results are shown)
Company research (happens during scrape, before results are shown)
CV tailoring (happens after Stage 1 approval, user sees spinner)
Tier 1-2 answer auto-fill (happens during tailoring, from vault)
Tier 3-4 answer generation (happens during tailoring, via API)
Form filling (happens when user clicks Submit, via Playwright)

streamlit_implementation_notes.multi_page: Use Streamlit's built-in multi-page app structure (pages/ directory) or st.navigation for the sidebar.
streamlit_implementation_notes.state_management: Use st.session_state for in-page state (edit mode, expanded cards). Database is the source of truth for all persistent data.
streamlit_implementation_notes.pdf_display: Use st.download_button for CV and cover letter PDFs. Optionally render inline with an iframe if Streamlit supports it.
streamlit_implementation_notes.synchronous_execution: Everything runs synchronously in the user's session. When user clicks 'Scrape now', the scraper runs and the UI shows a spinner until complete. When user clicks 'Submit', Playwright runs and the UI shows a spinner until submission succeeds or fails. No background threads, no async workers.
streamlit_implementation_notes.page_refresh: Use st.rerun() after state-changing actions (approve, skip, submit, save settings) to refresh the current page.
streamlit_implementation_notes.styling: Use st.markdown with custom CSS for card layouts, badges, and status colours. Streamlit's default theme is clean enough — don't over-customise.
streamlit_implementation_notes.startup_script: Create run.sh (Mac/Linux) and run.bat (Windows) that launch Streamlit and open the browser. User double-clicks to start. No terminal needed.
